content.text: The PO-33 uses Teenage Engineering's proprietary PO sync system via the 3.5mm stereo jack. This allows multiple Pocket Operators to play in sync, with clock on one channel and audio on the other.
The sync port can operate in different modes depending on whether the PO-33 is sending or receiving clock.

rows: PO-33 sends clock, other devices follow
Like SY1 but with input level control
Like SY2 but with output level control

items: While holding, press pads 1-5 to select sync mode (SY0-SY5)

content.caption: Sync port and BPM knob for sync settings

content.text: To sync multiple Pocket Operators together, connect them in a chain using stereo 3.5mm cables.

items: Set the first PO to SY1 (sync output / master)
Set the last PO to SY2 (sync input / slave)
Connect from each PO's sync port to the next
Press play on the master - all units start together

content.title: Cable Tips
content.text: Use stereo (TRS) cables for sync connections. Mono cables will only carry clock, not audio. A stereo Y-splitter lets you send audio to headphones while still syncing.

items: Clock runs at 8 pulses per quarter note (8 PPQ)
Left channel carries sync clock signal
Right channel carries mono audio output

content.text: The PO sync format is not directly compatible with standard MIDI or analog sync. However, converter devices like the Teenage Engineering POM-400 or third-party solutions can bridge the formats.

content.title: Volca Sync
content.text: Korg Volca series uses a similar sync format (2 PPQ) and can sometimes be connected with varying results. A sync converter is recommended for reliable timing.
